businessSummary.oneLiner: World's largest EV maker and 'Physical AI Platform' spanning autonomous driving, robotics, energy, and AI chips
businessSummary.description: Tesla extends beyond EV sales into FSD autonomy, Cybercab robotaxi, Optimus humanoid robot, and Megapack energy storage. FY2025 revenue hit $94.8B with GAAP EPS of $1.08, and Q4 gross margin reached 20.1%, a 2-year high. FSD has shifted to a $99/month subscription model, and April 2026 brings Cybercab production and Roadster unveil as key catalysts.
howTheyMakeMoney: Energy storage and generation (Megapack, Powerwall, Solar)
Services and charging (Supercharger network, insurance, OTA upgrades)

keyMetrics.name: P/E (TTM)
keyMetrics.interpretation: Extreme premium vs S&P 500 avg ~23x and auto sector avg ~12x. Still at astronomical multiples despite 27% decline from 52-week high

keyMetrics.name: Forward P/E
keyMetrics.interpretation: Based on 2026 EPS estimate of $2.08. Over 4x the growth stock avg of 30-40x. Prices in explosive robotaxi and energy growth

keyMetrics.name: Revenue Growth (YoY)
keyMetrics.interpretation: FY2025 revenue $94.8B, barely grew YoY. FY2026 expected to grow 15% to $108.9B

keyMetrics.name: Market Cap
keyMetrics.interpretation: YTD -20%. Still larger than Toyota, VW, GM, Ford combined but shrinking rapidly

keyMetrics.name: RSI (14-day)
keyMetrics.interpretation: Approaching oversold threshold of 30. Extreme selling pressure but technical bounce possible

growthDrivers.title: Tesla captures 50%+ EV market share amid 28% industry decline (3/27)
growthDrivers.description: While the overall EV market plunged 28%, Tesla expanded market share above 50%. Brand power and Supercharger infrastructure provide competitive advantage during downturns

growthDrivers.title: SpaceX IPO rumors emerge as valuation catalyst (3/25)
growthDrivers.description: Rumors of SpaceX IPO preparations could serve as an indirect catalyst for Tesla investors and trigger a broader Musk ecosystem revaluation

growthDrivers.title: Optimus3 robot video reveal, summer production start (3/25)
growthDrivers.description: Tesla unveiled Optimus3 humanoid robot's reduction gear and precision hand design. Targeting 1M units annual production rate by year-end. Trefis values the robotics division at $2-3T

growthDrivers.title: April catalyst cluster: Cybercab production + Roadster reveal
growthDrivers.description: Steerless Cybercab robotaxi mass production and next-gen Roadster reveal both scheduled for April, creating concentrated catalysts

growthDrivers.title: Terafab $25B AI chip factory, Wedbush calls it 'game changer'
growthDrivers.description: Wedbush reiterated $600 target, calling Terafab semiconductor factory a 'game changer'. AI5 chip low-volume production starts late 2026

growthDrivers.title: NHTSA escalates FSD investigation (3/19)
growthDrivers.description: NHTSA upgraded its probe into FSD performance in low-visibility conditions. Trefis warns this could break the '$1T AI thesis'

competitiveAdvantage.summary: 50%+ US EV market share, 8.4B miles of FSD data, NACS Supercharger standard dominance, AI chip internalization via Terafab

moats.description: 8.4 billion miles of real-world FSD driving data. Scale that competitors cannot replicate in the short term
Supercharger network adopted as NACS standard. Ford, GM and others use Tesla's charging infrastructure
Battery cells, AI chips (Terafab), software, charging infra all vertically integrated for cost and innovation advantages
Synonymous with EVs. High brand awareness and passionate fanbase drive demand without marketing spend

competitors.name: BYD
competitors.detail: China #1, 100%+ growth in Europe. Rapidly closing the gap on price competitiveness

competitors.name: Zoox (Amazon)
competitors.detail: Expanding robotaxi in SF, Las Vegas. Direct robotaxi competitor

competitors.name: Waymo (Alphabet)
competitors.detail: Ahead of Tesla in commercial autonomous ride-hailing. 200K+ paid rides per week

competitors.name: Hyundai/Kia
competitors.detail: Growing global EV share with Ioniq and EV6. Competing on value and quality

recentNews.headline: 'No Kings' protests draw 8M+ participants worldwide; 200+ anti-Tesla demonstrations
recentNews.significance: 3,300+ events globally with 8M+ attendees. Tesla became a direct protest target with 200+ anti-Tesla demonstrations. Musk's DOGE political involvement deepens brand damage, with European Jan registrations already down 17%

recentNews.headline: Musk seeks jury-bias probe in Twitter case, claims '$4.20 mockery'
recentNews.significance: Musk's legal team requested a federal judge review the Twitter acquisition verdict, arguing jurors mocked him. Adds governance distractions and legal overhang

recentNews.headline: HSBC maintains $131 target, warns of '65% further downside'
recentNews.significance: HSBC keeps Reduce rating at $131. An extreme bear case gaining attention as sentiment worsens

recentNews.headline: Tesla captures 50%+ EV market share as total EV market drops 28%
recentNews.significance: Tesla expanded share even as the broader EV market contracted sharply. Competitive moat validated in downturn

recentNews.headline: Judge dismisses X Corp advertising boycott lawsuit
recentNews.significance: Musk's X Corp antitrust lawsuit against advertisers dismissed by federal judge. Another legal setback raising governance questions

analystOpinions.notableComment: Of 43 analysts: 15 Strong Buy, 2 Buy, 17 Hold, 9 Strong Sell. Median $473 (+30% upside). Extreme bears: HSBC $131, Trip Chowdhry $150. Wedbush $600 high

risks.title: 'No Kings' protests: 8M people, 200+ anti-Tesla demonstrations
risks.description: 3,300+ protest events worldwide on 3/28 with 8M+ attendees. Tesla directly targeted at 200+ locations. European Jan registrations already -17%. Boycott movement now demonstrably impacting actual sales

risks.title: Below both 50-day and 200-day moving averages
risks.description: Trading below 50-day ($387) and 200-day ($400) MAs. RSI 33 approaching oversold but no trend reversal signal. 5 consecutive down days

risks.title: Extreme valuation risk
risks.description: TTM P/E 335x is 14.5x the S&P 500 average of 23x. Any growth disappointment could trigger sharp selloff

risks.title: Musk legal risks compounding
risks.description: Twitter jury bias claim, X boycott lawsuit dismissed. Consecutive legal defeats raise governance and management focus concerns

risks.title: Q1 delivery concerns intensifying
risks.description: Consensus 365,645 units is -13% vs Q4. HSBC considers even this overestimated

risks.title: FSD regulatory and safety risks
risks.description: NHTSA investigation escalated, California robotaxi application denied. Trefis warns the '$1T AI thesis could collapse'

buyReasons.title: Market share expansion during industry downturn proves competitive moat
buyReasons.rationale: 50%+ share while the EV market drops 28%. Dominance strengthens during downturns, positioning Tesla for maximum upside when market recovers

buyReasons.title: April catalyst cluster: Cybercab, Roadster, Q1 earnings (4/21)
buyReasons.rationale: Cybercab production, Roadster reveal, Netherlands FSD approval, and Q1 earnings all concentrated in April for potential momentum reversal

buyReasons.title: RSI 33, entering oversold territory
buyReasons.rationale: RSI approaching 30 oversold threshold. 27% discount from 52-week high. Rising probability of technical bounce

buyReasons.title: SpaceX IPO + Optimus robot as long-term revaluation catalysts
buyReasons.rationale: SpaceX IPO rumors and Optimus3 production start could justify Tesla's 'beyond automaker' valuation thesis long-term

overallOpinion: 'No Kings' protests drew 8M+ participants worldwide with 200+ anti-Tesla demonstrations. Musk's political activity has translated into boycotts that are now measurably hurting sales: European Jan registrations down 17%, and the movement is becoming entrenched

April brings concentrated catalysts: Cybercab production, Roadster reveal, FSD Europe approval, and Q1 earnings on 4/21. Tesla maintained 50%+ EV market share even as the market dropped 28%, proving its competitive moat
Musk's legal risks are compounding: Twitter jury bias claim, X boycott suit dismissed. HSBC $131 and Trip Chowdhry $150 bear cases gaining traction as sentiment deteriorates
$361.83, RSI 33 oversold. Below 50-day ($387) and 200-day ($400). YTD -20%. April catalysts are the key to reversal, but protest escalation is a new headwind. Hold, wait for Q1 earnings confirmation

investmentVerdict.summary: RSI 33 oversold and April catalyst concentration support a bounce case, but 8M-person protests and brand damage entrenchment go beyond mere sentiment. European -17% registration decline is concrete evidence. Wait for Q1 earnings (4/21) rather than panic sell, but protest escalation could further impact Q1 deliveries

witty.hook: 8M protests vs April catalysts: Tesla's impossible equation
witty.text:
$TSLA $361.83, YTD -20%

8M people protested. 200+ anti-Tesla rallies. EU sales -17%. Musk's political experiment, shareholders pay.

But EV market dropped 28% and Tesla's share rose above 50%. When everyone else falls, the last one standing wins.

April: Cybercab, Roadster, FSD Europe, earnings. Musk loses in court but the factories keep running.

RSI 33. Hold. Protests are loud, numbers are stubborn.

empathy.hook: TSLA shareholders, the protest headlines on top of -20% YTD must be exhausting
empathy.text:
$TSLA $361.83, down 27% from highs.

8M protesters, 200+ anti-Tesla rallies, YTD -20%. Stock pain plus headline pain. It's completely understandable to feel frustrated.

But Tesla held 50%+ EV share during a 28% market crash. April brings Cybercab, Roadster, and earnings (4/21).

RSI 33 means selling is near exhaustion. Don't panic sell. Wait for April. Protests are temporary, fundamentals are structural.
